problem: Two cubes with the faces numbered 1 through 6 are tossed and the numbers shown on the top faces are added. What is the probability that the sum is even? Express your answer as a common fraction.

classified_questions.question: How many different outcomes are possible when tossing two cubes with faces numbered 1 through 6?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the possible sums that can be obtained by adding the numbers shown on the top faces of the two cubes?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many of these possible sums would be considered even numbers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why there are three possible sums that are even and three possible sums that are odd?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: If we rolled a single cube, what would be the probability of getting an even number?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the answer to the previous question help us determine the probability of getting an even sum when rolling two cubes?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Is the probability of getting an even sum the same as the probability of getting an odd sum? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other scenarios or situations where the concept of even and odd numbers can be applied to probability calculations?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: How would the probability change if we were tossing three cubes instead of two?
classified_questions.label: b
